beats.n_start: {TOWN} on the night of {EVENT_NAME}, the one night a year the old {THRESHOLD} wakes; twelve-year-old {HERO}, chosen this year as the town's single new {ROLE_TITLE}, climbs the frost-bright lane to the {THRESHOLD_DESC}; as {HERO} reaches the door the whole building begins to hum and tick to life, dials spinning behind fogged glass; the stakes: at {DEADLINE} the Exchange must send the {SIGNAL} that keeps every clock, mill wheel, and school bell in the valley honest for the coming year, and tonight the signal is in trouble
beats.n_arrival: under the lobby's enormous dial, {NIGHTMASTER}, spry and inkstained, greets {HERO} and explains the {ROLE_TITLE}'s charge: each keeper minds one hall of {THRESHOLD} through the {EVENT_NAME} night and keeps its part of the machine ready for the {SIGNAL}; tonight, {NIGHTMASTER} says gravely, something has gone wrong in every one of the four halls at once, and he cannot mind them all; he presses a {KEYRING} into {HERO}'s hand and says the choosing of a hall is the keeper's own, made once and kept till dawn
beats.n_sort: {NIGHTMASTER} names the four halls and their trouble this {EVENT_NAME}: {T1_HALL}, where the {T1_ARTIFACT} drags and {DEADLINE} will come late unless the mechanism is mended; {T2_HALL}, where a {T2_FALSEECHO} loops through the {T2_DOME} and the {T2_ARTIFACT} must be found before the wrong one is sent; {T3_HALL}, the {T3_UNDERWORKS} whose water pressure drives the whole Exchange, now leaking toward a burst; and {T4_HALL}, the {T4_TUBEROOM}, where the {T4_CAPSULE} carrying the {T4_TOKEN} has jammed somewhere in the {T4_VEINS}; the halls will not share a keeper, {NIGHTMASTER} says, and the night is already turning
beats.t1_root: the spiral stair to {T1_HALL}, cold and loud with brass; {T1_MENTOR}, the retired clocksmith who tends the loft, waits by the {T1_ARTIFACT}, which swings a shade too slow; she tells {HERO} the escapement is worn and {DEADLINE} will fall late unless they win back the lost minutes before the hour
beats.t1_intro: {T1_MENTOR} opens the movement's glass and shows {HERO} the trouble: a {T1A_TOOL} on the escapement, a {T1B_TOOL} with a loose pin, and a pendulum bob whose trim has crept; any one of the three, mended right, could steady the tick in time
beats.t1_survey: {HERO} reads the loft's frost-fogged gauges while {T1_MENTOR} lays out her tools; the slow tick knocks in the dark like a tired heart, and the hour hand crawls toward {DEADLINE} that will not wait for a clumsy repair
beats.t1_hub: {T1_MENTOR} says a keeper mends one fault well rather than three badly; the {T1A_TOOL} can be re-cut at the bench, the jammed {T1B_TOOL} freed by climbing the frame, or the dragging {T1_ARTIFACT} set right by rebalancing its heavy bob
beats.t1_a1: at the bench {HERO} files a new bearing face onto the {T1A_TOOL} under {T1_MENTOR}'s eye, slow strokes, checking the fit against the escape wheel again and again until the shape comes true beneath the lamp
beats.t1_a2: the {T1A_TOOL} is cut but soft; {T1_MENTOR} offers two roads: harden it properly in the quench and lose precious minutes, or fit it green and gamble that soft steel holds through the single stroke of {DEADLINE}
beats.t1_aa1: {HERO} heats and quenches the {T1A_TOOL}, counting breaths against the falling hour; the steel hisses, hardens, and comes out keen; {T1_MENTOR} fits it while the loft holds its breath
beats.t1_aa2: the escapement catches the new {T1A_TOOL}, releases, catches; the slow knock quickens, evens, and climbs back to a true, bright tick; {T1_MENTOR}'s hands go still and the hour hand steadies exactly on its mark
beats.t1_aa_end: at {DEADLINE} {T1_HALL} strikes clean and on time, and {HERO} feels the whole valley's clocks lean into the true signal; {T1_MENTOR} says the loft has not ticked so honestly in years, and tells {HERO} a keeper who mends slow keeps well
beats.t1_ab1: {HERO} fits the green {T1A_TOOL} and the tick steadies, but {T1_MENTOR} frowns at the soft new steel; for an hour it holds beautifully, then, minutes before {DEADLINE}, the {T1A_TOOL} burrs and the tick begins to limp again
beats.t1_ab_end: {HERO} and {T1_MENTOR} hand-ease the movement through the hour and {T1_HALL} sends its signal a few minutes late; the valley's clocks will run a touch behind all year, a small honest error; {T1_MENTOR} says green steel and haste make their own kind of time, and next {EVENT_NAME} they will do it slow
beats.t1_b1: {HERO} climbs into the loft's iron frame toward the jammed {T1B_TOOL}, boots on cold girders, the pendulum sweeping past close enough to stir the air
beats.t1_b2: clinging to the frame, {HERO} finds the fault: a {T1B_PIN} has wedged between two teeth, and behind it, scratched into the metal, are the {T1B_MARKS}
beats.t1_b3: with a pick and a steady hand {HERO} eases the {T1B_PIN} free; the {T1B_TOOL} shivers and remembers how to turn, and {T1_MENTOR} calls up that the tick is gathering strength
beats.t1_b4: safe on the boards again, {HERO} finds a {T1B_LOGBOOK} {T1_MENTOR} had never shown, each mended {EVENT_NAME} recorded in a different hand; the {T1B_TOOL} runs true and {DEADLINE} rings clean while {HERO} reads a century of keepers by lamplight
beats.t1_b_end: {DEADLINE} strikes on time, and {T1_MENTOR} lets {HERO} scratch a new set of initials beside the old ones in the frame; the trouble was only a fallen pin, but {HERO} leaves the loft knowing {T1_HALL} is really a chain of hands, each keeping the time for the next, and now {HERO}'s hand is one of them
beats.t1_c1: {HERO} takes the pendulum road, sorting {T1_MENTOR}'s {T1C_TOOL} by the gram, each meant to nudge the heavy bob a hair faster without throwing off its long, patient swing
beats.t1_c2: weight by weight the sweep quickens toward true; {T1_MENTOR} teaches {HERO} to read the swing by ear, and warns that past a certain point more weight makes the bob wild instead of fast
beats.t1_c3: the tick sits almost perfect; {T1_MENTOR} says trust the arithmetic and lock the weights now, or chase the last hair of error and add one more trim past her warning
beats.t1_cc1: {HERO} locks the trim collars exactly where the numbers say and steps back; {T1_MENTOR} counts the swing under her breath and nods, once
beats.t1_cc2: the great bob settles into a long, even, unhurried sweep, and the tick rides it home; the hour hand walks up to its mark and stands there, patient and exact, as the loft warms with the sound of a clock that knows itself
beats.t1_cc_end: at {DEADLINE} {T1_HALL} leads the valley true, and {NIGHTMASTER} climbs up to mark in the great register that {EVENT_NAME} was kept; {T1_MENTOR} tells {HERO} that knowing when to stop is the whole craft, and that the loft has found itself a keeper it can trust with time
beats.t1_cd1: {HERO} adds the extra weight past {T1_MENTOR}'s warning; the bob lunges, the swing goes long and heavy and wrong, and a trim collar shakes loose and drops into the gears with a shriek
beats.t1_cd_end: {HERO}'s sleeve snags on the plunging bob and {T1_MENTOR} hauls {HERO} clear by the collar just in time; both are safe, but the seized movement cannot be freed before the hour, and {T1_HALL} stays silent at {DEADLINE}; the valley will set its clocks by guess this year, and {T1_MENTOR} holds {HERO} a long, shaken moment before she speaks
beats.t2_root: {T2_HALL} is a great dark {T2_DOME} hung with brass horns; {T2_MENTOR}, the gallery's half-deaf echo-reader, meets {HERO} with a lamp; a {T2_FALSEECHO} is looping somewhere in the dome, he says, and if the wrong message rides the {DEADLINE} signal the whole valley will keep a lie all year
beats.t2_intro: {T2_MENTOR} explains the gallery's craft: real messages arrive as clean echoes read off the {T2_FOCUSSTONE} in the dome's center, but tonight a garbled voice repeats and repeats, hiding the true one; three ways to run it down, he says, and only one night to try
beats.t2_survey: {HERO} stands on the {T2_FOCUSSTONE} while whispers slide down the dome from every side, some true, some false, all overlapping; picking the real message from the loop will take a cool head and a careful ear
beats.t2_hub: {T2_MENTOR} lays out the choices: trace the {T2_FALSEECHO} to its source horn on the gallery floor, sit the {T2_FOCUSSTONE} and sift the true words out by patient listening, or climb the high catwalk to the {T2C_TOOL} where every echo is born
beats.t2_a1: {HERO} walks the gallery floor, ear to one brass horn after another, following the false voice as it grows louder toward one shadowed alcove
beats.t2_a2: the {T2_FALSEECHO} pours from a {T2A_TOOL} stuffed with an old felt baffle; {T2_MENTOR} signs two choices: pull the baffle and silence the horn outright, or leave it and re-aim the horn so its lie folds back into itself
beats.t2_aa1: {HERO} swings the heavy horn on its pivot by tiny degrees, {T2_MENTOR} reading the dome with a hand flat on the stone, until the false voice meets its own returning wave
beats.t2_aa2: at the exact angle the lie erases itself, and out of the sudden quiet rises the {T2_ARTIFACT}, clear and whole: the valley's time, spoken plain; {T2_MENTOR} laughs a rusty laugh and copies it down
beats.t2_aa_end: the {T2_ARTIFACT} rides the {DEADLINE} signal and the valley keeps a whole honest year; {NIGHTMASTER} records {EVENT_NAME} kept, and {T2_MENTOR} tells {HERO} that anyone can make noise but a keeper learns to unmake it, and that the gallery would be glad of such ears again
beats.t2_ab1: {HERO} yanks the felt baffle and the false horn dies, but so does part of the dome's balance; the {T2_ARTIFACT} is there now, but thin and hard to hold, and {T2_MENTOR} must guess at two blurred words
beats.t2_ab_end: between them they piece out most of the message and send it, guessing the last of it right by luck and sense; the valley keeps good time, and {T2_MENTOR} teaches {HERO} that silencing a lie is easy but recovering the truth underneath is the real work, a lesson {HERO} turns over all the way down the stairs
beats.t2_b1: {HERO} sits cross-legged on the cold {T2_FOCUSSTONE} and closes both eyes, letting the whole dome pour its whispers down at once instead of chasing any single one
beats.t2_b2: slowly the loop reveals its shape: the false voice always lags a half-beat and never breathes, while the {T2_ARTIFACT}, faint beneath it, keeps a living rhythm; {HERO} learns to hear the difference
beats.t2_b3: holding the true rhythm like a thread, {HERO} follows it word by word out of the tangle while {T2_MENTOR} writes down each one {HERO} calls; the false voice rages louder but cannot break the thread
beats.t2_b4: the {T2_ARTIFACT} stands complete on {T2_MENTOR}'s slate, every word earned by listening; {T2_MENTOR} seals it for the sender, and the {T2_FALSEECHO}, unheeded at last, fades to nothing in the dome above
beats.t2_b_end: the honest message goes out at {DEADLINE} and the valley runs true; {T2_MENTOR} tells {HERO} that most keepers try to shout the lie down, and that {HERO} instead learned to listen it apart, which is the rarer gift; he presses a small brass ear-horn into {HERO}'s hand to remember the night
beats.t2_c1: {HERO} climbs the iron catwalk that rings the dome toward the {T2C_TOOL}, the false echo growing colder and louder with every rung, the gallery floor dropping away below into dark
beats.t2_c2: on the high walk the false voice is almost a person, wheedling, telling {HERO} the safe rail is rusted and the true horn lies out past it on the old unsafe gantry, just there, only a few steps beyond the barrier
beats.t2_c3: {T2_MENTOR}'s warning shout comes thin from below: stay inside the rail; the false echo insists the true horn is just past the barrier on the old gantry; {HERO} must choose whom to trust, the friend below or the voice in the dark
beats.t2_cc1: {HERO} keeps a hand on the safe rail and ignores the coaxing; from inside the barrier the {T2C_TOOL} is plainly reachable, and the false voice was only trying to lure a climber out over the drop
beats.t2_cc2: at the {T2C_TOOL} {HERO} finds the loose valve feeding the loop and closes it; the false voice snuffs out, and the whole dome fills with the single true message it had been drowning all night
beats.t2_cc_end: the recovered message rides the {DEADLINE} signal and the valley keeps true; climbing down, {HERO} understands the false echo was the gallery testing whether a keeper could tell a coaxing lie from a friend's warning, and {T2_MENTOR} says {HERO} passed the only test that ever mattered up there
beats.t2_cd1: {HERO} swings a leg over the barrier onto the old gantry, chasing the voice; the rusted iron groans, the promised horn is not there at all, only more dark, and the walkway tilts underfoot with a long tearing sound
beats.t2_cd_end: the tilting gantry pins {HERO} against the cold rail, boots slipping over nothing, the promised horn never there at all; the false voice goes gleeful and then, cheated of a fall, sullen, while {HERO} clings in the swallowing dark, too turned around to find the way back; only {T2_MENTOR}'s lamp and steady called-out directions bring {HERO} inching off the ruined walkway to safety, long minutes gone; the gallery keeps its oldest, saddest lesson, that a voice which flatters you toward the edge was never a friend, and {T2_HALL} sends no true message this {EVENT_NAME}
beats.t3_root: down slick stone stairs to {T3_HALL}, the {T3_UNDERWORKS} whose water pressure drives the whole Exchange; {T3_MENTOR} the cistern-keeper wades knee-deep with a storm-lantern; a seam is leaking, she says, and if the pressure drops before {DEADLINE} the signal upstairs dies for want of breath
beats.t3_intro: {T3_MENTOR} shows {HERO} the works: the great header tank, the pressure gauge creeping down, and a hairline leak somewhere along the old brick main; fix the pressure in time, she says, and the Exchange breathes; fail, and {DEADLINE} goes quiet
beats.t3_survey: cold water tugs at {HERO}'s boots while the gauge needle sinks; {T3_MENTOR} counts three ways to save the pressure before the hour, each with its own risk in the dark and the rising damp
beats.t3_hub: {T3_MENTOR} lays it out: dive to find and plug the leaking seam at the main, work the hand-pumps to refill the header tank faster than it drains, or shut the {T3C_TOOL} to seal off the leaking section entirely
beats.t3_a1: {HERO} takes a breath and goes under along the brick main, feeling for the pull of escaping water, {T3_MENTOR}'s lantern a wobbling green coin above the surface
beats.t3_a2: {HERO} finds the seam, a finger-width crack sucking cold; two choices surface with the next breath: pack it fast with {T3_MENTOR}'s {T3A_WOOL}, or take longer to set a proper {T3A_CLAMP} that will hold far better if the hour allows
beats.t3_aa1: diving again and again, {HERO} fits the {T3A_CLAMP} across the seam and draws it tight bolt by bolt, lungs burning, {T3_MENTOR} timing each dive from above and hauling {HERO} up to breathe between
beats.t3_aa2: the {T3A_CLAMP} bites and the sucking stops; the gauge needle shivers, halts, and climbs; the great tank stops draining and the Exchange draws a deep, even breath through the pipes overhead
beats.t3_aa_end: at {DEADLINE} {T3_HALL} holds full pressure and the signal goes out strong; {T3_MENTOR} wraps {HERO} in a dry coat and says most folk fear going under the cold water, and that a keeper who faces it and fixes it right belongs down here whenever the valley needs one
beats.t3_ab1: {HERO} jams the {T3A_WOOL} into the crack and the worst of the leak stops, but the fast patch weeps a slow thread of water that {T3_MENTOR} eyes with doubt as the gauge creeps only halfway back
beats.t3_ab_end: the patch holds just enough; {T3_HALL} sends its signal thin but real, and the valley keeps time on a shallow breath this year; {T3_MENTOR} tells {HERO} a fast plug beats a burst main any night, but that next {EVENT_NAME} they will set the {T3A_CLAMP} and do it properly, and {HERO} means to be there
beats.t3_b1: {HERO} throws off a coat and takes the long iron handle of the {T3B_TOOL}, and {T3_MENTOR} takes the other; together they begin the brutal rhythm of hauling water up faster than the leak can steal it
beats.t3_b2: the tank rises a hand's width, then holds; {HERO}'s arms turn to rope, but {T3_MENTOR} calls the stroke and the two of them find a pace they can just barely keep against the sinking gauge
beats.t3_b3: as {DEADLINE} nears the leak slows on its own, silt drawn into the crack, and the {T3B_TOOL} at last gains on it; the gauge needle lifts steadily under {HERO}'s aching, streaming effort
beats.t3_b4: the header tank brims and overflows its mark; the Exchange breathes deep and even, and {T3_MENTOR} finally lets the handle rest, grinning through the sweat at a job done by nothing but two backs and a lot of will
beats.t3_b_end: {T3_HALL} holds and the {DEADLINE} signal rings full and true; {NIGHTMASTER} comes down to record {EVENT_NAME} kept, and {T3_MENTOR} tells him the new keeper out-pumped a leak with bare arms; some nights, she says to {HERO}, the Exchange runs on muscle and stubbornness, and {HERO} has both
beats.t3_c1: {HERO} wades toward the {T3C_TOOL}, a rusted wheel as tall as a door, that will seal the whole leaking section away from the rest of the works if it can be turned
beats.t3_c2: the wheel is seized with rust and years; {T3_MENTOR} warns that shutting it saves the pressure but strands the far pumps, so it must be turned fully or not at all, no half measures in the cold dark
beats.t3_c3: the valve gives an inch and stops; {T3_MENTOR} says brace it slow and steady and it will come, or throw all of {HERO}'s weight against it at once and risk snapping the old spindle for good
beats.t3_cc1: {HERO} and {T3_MENTOR} lean into the wheel together, a hand's turn at a time, letting the old iron remember its threads instead of forcing them
beats.t3_cc2: with a groan and then a rush the valve seats home; the leaking section falls silent and the pressure everywhere else surges back; the gauge climbs past its mark and steadies, and the Exchange breathes easy
beats.t3_cc_end: the sealed works hold pressure to {DEADLINE} and the signal goes out true; reading the old valve's maker-mark by lantern, {HERO} learns {T3_HALL} was built by the same hands that raised {T1_HALL}, one machine breathing through the whole hill, and {T3_MENTOR} says a keeper who sees the whole works has seen the real Exchange
beats.t3_cd1: {HERO} throws everything into the wheel at once; it lurches a quarter-turn and the old spindle shears with a crack, jamming the valve half-open, neither sealed nor free, water pushing through the gap
beats.t3_cd_end: with the valve jammed half-shut the pressure cannot hold, and {T3_HALL} sends no signal from the underworks this {EVENT_NAME}; {HERO} and {T3_MENTOR} are cold and safe but beaten, and the valley will keep rough time this year; forcing the old iron, {T3_MENTOR} says quietly, only ever breaks it, a lesson {HERO} carries up the wet stairs
beats.t4_root: {T4_HALL} is a warm brass-veined room where message capsules whistle through pneumatic tubes; {T4_MENTOR}, the young tube-runner, meets {HERO} wide-eyed; the {T4_CAPSULE} carrying the {T4_TOKEN} has jammed somewhere in the veins, she says, and without it the sender has nothing true to send
beats.t4_intro: {T4_MENTOR} shows {HERO} the {T4_BOARD}, a wall of brass tubes and levers mapping the whole Exchange; the {T4_CAPSULE} is stuck somewhere between here and the sending station, and three routes might reach it before the hour
beats.t4_survey: {HERO} traces the cold dead tube where the capsule went silent; {T4_MENTOR} counts the ways to reach it, each running through a different stretch of the old {T4_VEINS}, each with its own hazard
beats.t4_hub: {T4_MENTOR} lays out the routes: crawl the service duct to the jam and clear it by hand, reroute the air pressure to blow the capsule free from the {T4_BOARD}, or send a {T4C_TOOL} down the tube to knock the stuck one loose
beats.t4_a1: {HERO} wriggles into the tight brass service duct with a hand-lamp, following the dead tube by touch toward the muffled place where the capsule sits wedged
beats.t4_a2: {HERO} reaches the jam: the capsule is crumpled against a {T4A_TOOL}; two choices in the cramped dark, ease the flap open gently and free the capsule whole, or shove hard and trust speed over care
beats.t4_aa1: {HERO} works the stiff {T4A_TOOL} open a careful degree at a time until the dented capsule slides loose into waiting fingers, its {T4_TOKEN} safe and dry inside
beats.t4_aa2: out of the duct, {HERO} loads the rescued capsule into the sending tube; {T4_MENTOR} throws the lever and it whistles away clean toward the station, the token intact and the minutes just barely enough
beats.t4_aa_end: the {T4_TOKEN} reaches the sender and the {DEADLINE} signal goes out whole; {NIGHTMASTER} marks {EVENT_NAME} kept, and {T4_MENTOR} tells {HERO} that runners learn the veins with their hands in the dark, and that {HERO} has the touch for it; {T4_HALL} would keep {HERO} gladly
beats.t4_ab1: {HERO} shoves the capsule hard through the flap; it tears free but the dented case springs open, and the {T4_TOKEN} skitters loose into the dark bottom of the duct
beats.t4_ab_end: {HERO} wedges deeper after the token and gets stuck fast in the narrowing brass, and {T4_MENTOR} has to fetch {NIGHTMASTER} and a grease-can to work {HERO} gently out; everyone is safe, but the token is lost below and {T4_HALL} sends no signal this {EVENT_NAME}; haste in a tight place, {T4_MENTOR} says, only ever wedges you tighter, and she stays close while they free {HERO}
beats.t4_b1: {HERO} stays at the {T4_BOARD} to reroute the air, and {T4_MENTOR} coaches the trick: close every branch but the jammed one so the whole Exchange's pressure gathers behind the stuck capsule at once
beats.t4_b2: lever by lever the {T4_BOARD} goes quiet as tubes fall shut, the gauges climbing as pressure banks up behind the jam; {T4_MENTOR} watches the dial and tells {HERO} when to hold
beats.t4_b3: at the right instant {HERO} throws the release; a great breath of air punches down the dead tube, and far off in the brass something knocks, shudders, and begins to move
beats.t4_b4: the {T4_CAPSULE} comes whistling home to the {T4_BOARD}, shaken loose and whole; {T4_MENTOR} catches it, whoops, and slots it straight into the sending tube while the last minutes drain away
beats.t4_b_end: the capsule reaches the sender and the signal goes out on time; {T4_MENTOR} tells {NIGHTMASTER} that {HERO} cleared the whole vein without ever leaving the board, just by understanding how the air wanted to move; a keeper who thinks the machine through, {NIGHTMASTER} says, is worth three who only climb into it, and he shakes {HERO}'s hand
beats.t4_c1: {HERO} loads a heavy {T4C_TOOL} to send down the tube after the jammed one, meaning to knock it loose the quick and simple way, and {T4_MENTOR} sets the pressure for the shot
beats.t4_c2: {T4_MENTOR} warns that a {T4C_TOOL} is a blunt tool: sent right it taps the jam free, sent too hard it drives both capsules into a worse wreck deeper in the {T4_VEINS} where no hand can reach
beats.t4_c3: the tube is loaded and {HERO}'s hand is on the lever; {T4_MENTOR} says send it soft and true, a gentle tap that might need a second try, or send it hard and settle the whole thing in one shot if it works
beats.t4_cc1: {HERO} feathers the lever and the {T4C_TOOL} slips away with a soft whoosh; it coasts down the tube and meets the jam with the lightest of taps, exactly as {T4_MENTOR} hoped
beats.t4_cc2: the tap is enough; the {T4_CAPSULE} shakes loose and both capsules come coasting back to the {T4_BOARD} unhurt, the {T4_TOKEN} rattling safe inside; {T4_MENTOR} grabs it and races it to the sending tube
beats.t4_cc_end: the signal goes out true at {DEADLINE}; sorting the two returned capsules, {HERO} finds the {T4C_TOOL} was an old training round from {T4_MENTOR}'s own first {EVENT_NAME}, her initials scratched inside, and {T4_MENTOR} goes quiet and glad; {T4_HALL} remembers its runners, she says, and now it will remember {HERO} too
beats.t4_cd1: {HERO} slams the lever full; the heavy {T4C_TOOL} rockets down the tube and hits the jam with a bang that shakes the whole {T4_BOARD}, and both capsules crumple together deep in an unreachable bend of the brass
beats.t4_cd_end: the wrecked capsules sit jammed where no runner can reach before dawn, and {T4_HALL} sends nothing this {EVENT_NAME}; {HERO} and {T4_MENTOR} are unhurt but low, and the valley will guess at its clocks this year; a {T4C_TOOL} sent in anger, {T4_MENTOR} says, only ever makes two jams out of one, and {HERO} nods, learning it the hard way

n_start.c_n_start_1: Push open the {ENTRANCE_DOOR} and go in.

t1_hub.c_t1_hub_2: Climb the frame to free the jammed {T1B_TOOL}.

t1_aa1.c_t1_aa1_1: Set the hardened {T1A_TOOL} and watch the tick.

t2_hub.c_t2_hub_1: Trace the {T2_FALSEECHO} to its source horn.
t2_hub.c_t2_hub_2: Sit the {T2_FOCUSSTONE} and sift the words by ear.

t2_a2.c_t2_a2_1: Re-aim the horn to cancel the {T2_FALSEECHO}.
t2_a2.c_t2_a2_2: Pull the baffle and silence the {T2A_TOOL}.

t2_c3.c_t2_c3_1: Trust {T2_MENTOR} and stay inside the rail.

t3_intro.c_t3_intro_1: Look over the failing works with {T3_MENTOR}.

t3_hub.c_t3_hub_3: Shut the {T3C_TOOL} on the leaking section.

t3_a2.c_t3_a2_1: Set the {T3A_CLAMP}, the strong slow fix.

t3_c3.c_t3_c3_1: Brace it slow and steady with {T3_MENTOR}.

t4_hub.c_t4_hub_3: Send a {T4C_TOOL} to knock it loose.

t4_a2.c_t4_a2_1: Ease the {T4A_TOOL} and free the capsule whole.

t4_c3.c_t4_c3_2: Send the {T4C_TOOL} hard, one shot to settle it.
